key_insight: A CF/PC rivet combined with ultrasonic hybrid riveting–welding and TC4 surface engineering creates a stronger dissimilar joint while enabling real-time resistance-based monitoring.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a fast, adhesive-free, and fastener-free joining route for lightweight CFRTP–titanium structures in aerospace and other high-performance transport systems.
method: Hot rivet a same-material CF/PC rivet into the joint
Ultrasonically weld CF/PC to Ti-6Al-4V in one cycle
Add TC4 grooving and anodic oxidation to improve interfacial bonding
Monitor interfacial resistance during welding and mechanical loading
evidence_signal: Compared with direct welding, joint shear strength increased by 11.08% and cross-lap tensile strength increased by 128.6%, with resistance stages tracking weld evolution and damage progression.
watch_item: The resistance-to-strength model is preliminary, so broader validation across geometries, loading modes, and production variability is still needed.
paper_title: Interfacial joining and in-situ resistance monitoring in ultrasonic hybrid riveting–welding of CFRTP and titanium alloy
